111712.explanation: We find it important to connect with what matters in Culemborg and to know the opinions of residents on certain topics. An advisory referendum can be a good tool for this, provided enough residents participate.

111715.explanation: Agriculture must become much more sustainable, but we also want farmers to earn money with their farms. A store selling good products from local land or a nice campsite can help with this. Therefore, we want to enable these options.

111716.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity in the same place, so the energy grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality.

111719.explanation: Those who bring workers to our municipality must ensure that these workers have decent housing, without causing inconvenience to our residents. There is no place in our municipality for exploitation and nuisance.

111721.explanation: We do not want an immediate ban, but want to phase out the use of fireplaces and stoves. Residents should be given sufficient time to prepare their homes for this.

111724.explanation: Collecting all bulky waste for free costs money that we would rather spend on other matters. However, for people who are unable to bring their bulky waste themselves and cannot arrange this within their network, we want to offer this service.

111729.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own area. It is one of the pillars of local democracy. The municipality supports local media. At this time, we do not have the financial means for additional support and are making other choices.

111732.explanation: Playing sports gives a lot of energy and satisfaction. It keeps you healthy and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club closes down, many residents suffer. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in when there are good reasons to do so.

111735.explanation: To prevent nuisance and crime, extra camera surveillance can be deployed. Of course, we support extra camera surveillance only when it is deployed very purposefully and privacy is safeguarded as much as possible.

111737.explanation: For many first-time buyers it is difficult to purchase a home. A starter’s loan from the municipality makes the step towards owning a home feasible and also ensures housing market flow.

111740.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

111741.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the greenery around our residential area as well as possible. But to tackle the housing shortage, we must also use agricultural land for (small-scale) housing. This is also an opportunity to create nice, green neighborhoods for our residents.

111742.explanation: People on social assistance must have a chance to participate. Therefore, we invest in social entrepreneurship and in guiding people towards (volunteer) work. We operate with trust in people.

111744.explanation: We help residents with financial problems as early as possible. This often prevents (even greater) debts. Not only income should be considered, but also the situation someone is in. Combating poverty requires a tailor-made approach.

111747.explanation: The figures clearly show: with a lower maximum speed, traffic in our municipality becomes much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with mobility issues, this makes a world of difference. Furthermore, the streets are quieter and the air cleaner.

111748.explanation: Residents deserve appropriate and accessible care that suits their needs.

111750.explanation: The revenue from property tax is used, among other things, for the maintenance of roads, green spaces, social facilities, police, fire department, petting zoo, theater, swimming pool, and schools. This makes Culemborg a pleasant municipality to live in.

111751.explanation: This money is used for keeping public spaces clean and maintaining dog exercise fields. If we abolish the dog tax, these costs would still need to be covered.

111752.explanation: The costs the municipality incurs because of tourists can be paid by the tourists themselves.

111755.explanation: Municipalities are required to house a number of status holders determined by the national government. The only way to do this is by giving status holders priority. We want many more social rental homes and want other vulnerable groups to also be able to get housing.

111757.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

111758.explanation: The swimming pool must remain in use, which requires regular maintenance. This calls for investments.

111760.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and Culemborg has abundant water. Every child should learn to swim, including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not arranged fairly. The best solution: swimming lessons for every child at school, supported by the municipality.

111761.explanation: We currently have two coffee shops. That is sufficient for a city the size of Culemborg.

111763.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage and action is needed. With high-rise buildings, many homes can be added quickly. We say: build upwards near a good public transport connection. That way, we make good use of that precious land.

111764.explanation: Every neighborhood needs a community center. A place where people meet, ask questions, and get help from others. Here residents get to know each other.

111766.explanation: Parking requires a tailored approach. Some neighborhoods need more spaces, while others often have spaces left unused. We want to encourage car sharing.

111768.explanation: We support sustainable energy. Energy from wind turbines fits this. We favor a transparent and open process in which residents are well involved and where a location is chosen that suits the city.

111769.explanation: The petting zoo is an important place where people meet and it is accessible to everyone. There is much support in Culemborg to preserve the petting zoo.

111770.explanation: It makes sense that the bicycle bridge will be built. This will create a continuous bike route between Den Bosch, Culemborg, and Utrecht. This is great for Culemborg residents and good for tourism. The provincial and national governments should pay the majority of the costs.

111712.explanation: It sounds very nice, but it has not yet proven to be a well-developed effective measure.

111715.explanation: Not always, within the prevailing rules and laws this is of course possible.

111716.explanation: There are possibilities from the national government; the municipality cannot simply support every initiative.

111719.explanation: If they have the possibility and it can be done responsibly and legally, then that is fine. Otherwise, it should be done in other places where it is responsible.

111721.explanation: Within the rules it is allowed, but if you cause nuisance, then it is not allowed.

111724.explanation: This implies that a lot of bulky waste from renovations etc. must also be collected. However, under certain conditions large items can be collected on request.

111729.explanation: There is already a subsidy, and with extra money it might seem as if everything should be paid for.

111732.explanation: A sports club should be able to finance itself with membership fees and sponsors or other income. If that does not succeed, then the question is whether the club is viable. For example, the Culemborg standard does not include an ice hockey club.

111735.explanation: Camera surveillance is part of a good safety policy.

111737.explanation: The municipality is not a financier.

111740.explanation: It is not an issue in Culemborg and not recommended, but if it is possible without problems then it is fine.

111741.explanation: Housing is needed, but what is there in Culemborg outside the village boundary? We would like to build in the Station Quarter.

111742.explanation: That depends very much on the situation. You cannot see every social assistance recipient as the same. A tailored approach is necessary. Besides, it is a national issue.

111744.explanation: A boundary is a boundary. A national standard is set, which we should not raise.

111747.explanation: Not everywhere. That is not realistic.

111748.explanation: Yes, you should be able to choose, but if there is no choice then you must accept what is available.

111750.explanation: We would like that, but it is not always realistic in the current situation.

111751.explanation: There must be clearer policy on this point before abolishing it and it must not lead to an increase in property tax.

111752.explanation: It is at a good level and you also want to retain tourists.

111755.explanation: No priority, but work with a small housing stock to allow flow if possible.

111757.explanation: Not banning, but establishing good rules of play.

111758.explanation: No, it is not necessary, only the necessary maintenance.

111760.explanation: Stimulating and arrangements for social assistance recipients is fine.

111761.explanation: Assess within the rules, do not block in advance.

111763.explanation: If the housing shortage must be solved, then high residential towers may be built, with neat apartments for small households and singles.

111764.explanation: Community centers are fine, but whether every neighborhood should have one needs to be examined.

111766.explanation: There are enough parking spaces, not always directly at the door, but we do not start from the assumption that every household has two cars.

111768.explanation: Only within the agreed arrangements and without nuisance. Not all meadows are suitable for this.

111769.explanation: It must not cost extra money; it should actually be more self-sufficient.

111770.explanation: No pipe dreams, only realistic plans that are affordable.

111712.explanation: The most important task of a municipal council is to represent the people and listen to residents and entrepreneurs. An advisory referendum can contribute to this; the decision-making is for the municipal council.

111715.explanation: Farmers must be given the freedom to undertake business, especially now that there is so much pressure on farming livelihoods.

111716.explanation: The municipality must certainly facilitate sustainable energy. A prerequisite for responsible sustainable energy generation is that there is a positive business case. It is not the municipality's task to cover losses and/or risks in sustainable energy generation.

111719.explanation: It ensures that regular homes used for seasonal workers become available for Culemborg residents.

111721.explanation: There are not many complaints. We rely on the responsibility of our residents to limit nuisance.

111724.explanation: The current system works well. Large items are collected by appointment for 20 euros; smaller items can be brought in by residents for free.

111729.explanation: Especially if the broadcaster critically follows the politics and administration, thereby allowing room for independent investigative journalism.

111732.explanation: Sports are good for health, community spirit, and development in many areas. Culemborg is generous in contributions to facilities, and this should continue. The club is responsible for the rest of the financing.

111735.explanation: Camera surveillance demonstrably helps improve safety on the streets. Where camera surveillance disappears, as recently at Schoolhof, we immediately see a negative effect.

111737.explanation: It helps Culemborg's youth. Culemborg has limited financial space because many investments need to be made. In addition, school housing and residential construction have priority over starter loans.

111740.explanation: It is a solution to the housing need. The owner of the holiday park is responsible for ensuring it remains a livable neighborhood.

111741.explanation: New construction on the outskirts of the city is the fastest, with the fewest complications and can be realized at the lowest cost. Our young people do not have time to wait.

111742.explanation: Work, adapted if necessary, is the route out of benefit dependency, to actively participate in society and is good for mental and physical health.

111744.explanation: The municipality must help people find work. That is sustainable assistance.

111747.explanation: Only where it is affordable and contributes to safety.

111748.explanation: Care for everyone must be equitable. The multitude of care providers unfortunately leads to risks of healthcare fraud, inefficiency due to fragmentation, and results in higher costs and longer waiting times.

111750.explanation: Because the municipal organization has been spared in recent council terms, Culemborg is already at a maximum property tax contribution.

111751.explanation: The dog tax is a general tax and does not directly benefit dog owners. It is unfair that owning a dog leads to padding the municipal treasury in general.

111752.explanation: Visitors to Culemborg are important for our shopkeepers and hospitality sector.

111755.explanation: Status holders are received in modest housing (better than in a reception center but not yet social rental housing), we help them integrate into Culemborg and find work. For social rental housing, they must wait on the waiting list as long as Culemborg residents.

111757.explanation: It must fit into the environment. And if agricultural land is available for this, then place houses under the solar panels.

111758.explanation: A swimming pool is an important facility for our residents.

111760.explanation: This also benefits the operation of swimming pool De Waterlinie.

111761.explanation: Coffeeshops cause nuisance and contradict the responsible promotion of health.

111763.explanation: Preferably not, but it is necessary due to financial feasibility.

111764.explanation: For the size of Culemborg, one community center in the west and one in the east is sufficient. The spaces in a community center must be multifunctional.

111766.explanation: In many neighborhoods, parking pressure is too high, causing nuisance and unsafe situations. Homes should have enough parking space for residents and visitors, appropriate to how people live and work.

111768.explanation: Wind turbines in the fields are not economically feasible without health risks.

111769.explanation: The petting zoo must remain. However, possibilities for more revenue should be explored so that it costs the municipality less money.

111770.explanation: School housing, road maintenance, making roads safer, and residential construction are examples that are socially more important than a bicycle bridge.

111712.explanation: D66 Culemborg sees citizen participation as a better form of involving its residents.

111715.explanation: Good initiatives from residents should be given space.

111716.explanation: The municipality should facilitate the process, not fund it.

111719.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing.

111721.explanation: Any potential ban should be regulated nationally.

111724.explanation: This results in a disproportionate distribution of burdens.

111729.explanation: Local press ensures objective reporting on local issues.

111732.explanation: Sports clubs are primarily responsible for their financial management.

111740.explanation: The municipality must ensure good housing.

111741.explanation: We cherish our green rural area.

111742.explanation: Not every recipient of social assistance benefits is able to do so.

111744.explanation: The income limit is nationally standardized.

111747.explanation: Enforcing the maximum speed limit contributes more to safety.

111748.explanation: Healthcare is for everyone, regardless of religion, culture, or belief.

111750.explanation: The property tax (ozb) should bear the municipalities' costs and is not part of income policy.

111752.explanation: The tax must be proportional to the costs and is not a cash cow.

111755.explanation: Refugees must have equal opportunities as other housing seekers.

111758.explanation: The waterline is up to date.

111760.explanation: In a water-rich environment, swimming skills are essential.

111763.explanation: Infill development is a good solution for quickly increasing the housing supply.

111766.explanation: We support shared mobility and a lower parking standard.

111768.explanation: Sustainability is important. We need all energy sources.

111769.explanation: We support the autonomy of the Children's Farm. If financial support from the municipality is needed, we are willing to help under certain conditions.

111770.explanation: Many of our residents work across the river. We want to encourage bicycle use.

111712.explanation: A referendum is desirable on important issues that directly affect citizens. An example is wind turbines on Culemborg’s territory.

111715.explanation: We want to provide space for entrepreneurship and the future of farmers. Recreational options in the rural area are also part of this.

111716.explanation: Think, for example, of heat networks from the Lek river or neighborhood batteries.

111719.explanation: It is good that entrepreneurs are responsible for proper housing for their own employees.

111721.explanation: Air quality is a concern. We must adhere to the clean air agreement as much as possible.

111724.explanation: We must use our belongings sustainably and take care of their disposal ourselves.

111729.explanation: It is good that residents are informed about current local issues.

111732.explanation: Sport is important. Primarily, it is up to the club to generate sufficient income from members. If that is insufficient, the municipality can, for example, contribute through a loan.

111735.explanation: More cameras at strategic locations can help promote safety.

111737.explanation: There is not yet a starter loan in Culemborg. It must be established as soon as possible.

111741.explanation: Housing shortage requires us to look for solutions outside the usual frameworks.

111742.explanation: It is an aspiration, not an obligation.

111744.explanation: The help must be appropriate to the personal situation.

111747.explanation: Traffic safety is important for cyclists. We need to develop policies that specify where 30 km/h roads are allowed. The Stationssingel is an example.

111750.explanation: This fits with the principle that those with the greatest means bear the heaviest burdens.

111751.explanation: That is national policy and will happen.

111752.explanation: Tourist tax is currently 1.60 euros per person per night. That is low. In Utrecht, it is 8.5% of the overnight price.

111757.explanation: We must first focus on solar panels on roofs and insulation of homes and businesses.

111758.explanation: The costs are already far too high. We must carry out the necessary maintenance.

111760.explanation: No, this task is socially addressed through the Broad School Culemborg, Swimming Club De Meer, and Foundation Vrijstad Culemborg.

111763.explanation: Does not fit the scale of Culemborg. Our preference is 4-6 floors.

111766.explanation: Maintain the current parking standards and do not lower them further.

111768.explanation: There is insufficient support and the area is too small for large wind turbines. The existing three may remain.

111769.explanation: It is an important and educational meeting place for children and their parents.

111770.explanation: That is only possible if the national government and the provinces of Utrecht and Gelderland generously pay for it.

111712.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes residents deserve more say. Referendums can be a valuable additional tool for important decisions, provided they are well regulated, accessible, and accompanied by clear information for everyone.

111715.explanation: The Party for the Animals is not against secondary activities at farms, but 'always allowing' goes too far. Initiatives must fit with nature, landscape, animal welfare, and livability. The municipality must carefully weigh each case to determine what is responsible.

111716.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports residents who jointly produce sustainable energy. Local initiatives accelerate the energy transition, increase support, and ensure the benefits go fairly to residents, not to large polluting companies.

111719.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to stop abuses and exploitation of seasonal workers. Employers must provide safe, humane housing with strict requirements and enforcement, on their own grounds and if not possible, elsewhere. This protects workers and the livability of the city.

111721.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants clean air for everyone. Wood burning causes serious air pollution and health complaints, especially among children and vulnerable groups. We aim to phase out wood stoves and fireplaces and promote clean alternatives.

111724.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes bulky waste should be collected for free to prevent illegal dumping in public spaces. This is better for the environment, animals, and livability. We encourage reuse and repair, but easy collection remains necessary.

111729.explanation: The Party for the Animals considers a strong local broadcaster important for independent information, local democracy, and citizen engagement. Additional support helps keep journalism accessible, diverse, and reliable, by and for Culemborg residents.

111732.explanation: The Party for the Animals values sports clubs, but support is not automatic. The municipality can temporarily assist in exceptional circumstances or where there is social added value. Structural deficits or lack of enthusiasm are primarily the club's own responsibility.

111735.explanation: The Party for the Animals is cautious about camera surveillance. It infringes privacy and often does not prevent problems. Safety is mainly achieved through prevention, good lighting, social cohesion, and care. Camera surveillance may only be very temporary and demonstrably necessary.

111737.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to give starters a fair chance on the housing market. Due to rising house prices and interest rates, many people fall behind. A municipal starter loan can help even people with average incomes to keep living in Culemborg.

111740.explanation: The Party for the Animals understands the housing shortage, but permanent residence in vacation homes is not a sustainable solution. It leads to unsafe situations and undermines recreation and nature. The municipality should focus on real, affordable housing.

111741.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to use space sparingly. New construction outside existing village boundaries can harm landscape, nature, and biodiversity. We choose building within the city through reuse, quality densification, and preservation of quality green areas.

111742.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes people on social assistance have a right to subsistence security without coercion. Mandatory work often leads to exploitation and displaces paid work. We prefer investing in guidance, education, and suitable, voluntary work.

111744.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants Culemborg to expand minimum income policy to incomes up to 130% of the social minimum.

111747.explanation: With a maximum speed of 30 km/h in built-up areas, Culemborg becomes safer, cleaner, and more pleasant to live in. Road design will be adjusted where possible, creating space for greening.

111748.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes care should be people-centered. People should be able to choose a care provider who matches their background and experiences. This increases trust and quality of care, especially for groups who are currently too often not taken seriously.

111750.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants Culemborg to invest in people, animals, and nature. The property tax is a means to make this possible.

111751.explanation: Dog tax is spent on matters unrelated to dogs. Dog tax discriminates between dog owners and owners of other pets. This distinction is not justified.

111752.explanation: The Party for the Animals finds it logical that visitors contribute to facilities and livability. A higher tourist tax helps reduce pressure on the city and nature, and ensures that costs do not fall solely on residents.

111755.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes the municipality must fulfill its legal duty to house status holders. People with residence permits have the right to a safe place. The housing crisis is solved by building more social housing, not by pitting groups against each other.

111757.explanation: Solar panels and greenery should become the norm on roofs, facades, noise barriers, and above parking lots. Agricultural land is meant for plant-based food production and nature development, and if livestock farming shrinks significantly, for housing.

111758.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes swimming pool De Waterlinie must be renewed to be future-proof. No patchwork, but a thorough approach so the building is safe, sustainable, energy-efficient, and can last decades more for sport and health of everyone.

111760.explanation: The Party for the Animals considers school swimming essential for safety and equal opportunities. Every child must learn to swim, regardless of income. By funding school swimming, the municipality prevents drownings and increases health and confidence.

111761.explanation: The Party for the Animals opts for a realistic and humane drug policy. Regulation is better than prohibition. A coffeeshop can contribute to supervision, public health, and reducing illegal trade, provided it is well regulated and appropriately integrated. Preferably with organic plants, of course ;).

111763.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants affordable housing, but high-rise buildings are not a goal in themselves. Building must fit the city, with attention to livability, green space, biodiversity, and health. We choose quality, sustainability, and space for nature, not just building higher.

111764.explanation: The Party for the Animals sees neighborhood centers as important places for meeting, care, and cohesion. Not every neighborhood needs a large building, but there should be an accessible meeting place fitting the neighborhood and in cooperation with residents.

111766.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants fewer cars and more space for green areas, playgrounds, and safe bicycle paths. Additional parking spaces increase traffic pressure and take up scarce space. We prefer investing in walking, cycling, and good public transport.

111768.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants sustainable energy, but not at the expense of nature, landscape, and animals. We first choose energy savings, solar panels on roofs, and then wind energy in ways and places with minimal impact, such as along infrastructure and industrial areas.

111769.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to preserve Children's Farm De Heuvel, but not in its current form. Breeding, selling, and slaughtering animals must stop. The Party for the Animals wants a shelter and education place for animals in need, with space for natural behavior and animal welfare as a priority.

111770.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports better cycling connections and sees the added value of a cycle bridge over the Lek. Costs, nature, landscape, and alternatives must be carefully weighed.

111741.explanation: This statement is not relevant for Culemborg. Therefore, it does not apply.

111712.explanation: The CDA wants to better utilize the intellectual capacity of residents but believes there are cheaper ways to gather input from residents. We find the costs of a referendum too high. Also, some issues cannot easily be reduced to a simple yes-no question.

111715.explanation: The CDA wants residents to be able to recreate and farmers to run businesses in our rural area. This requires us to look at plans through a positive lens and with an open attitude. This was also agreed upon in the environmental vision.

111716.explanation: The CDA wants to take initiatives from society seriously. Therefore, residents can apply for national subsidies and local sustainability loans. It is the responsibility of the initiators themselves to come up with a good spatial/technical/financial plan.

111719.explanation: The CDA wants employers to take care of decent housing themselves, but this does not necessarily have to be on their own premises. This can also be fine elsewhere.

111721.explanation: The CDA wants to better protect residents from nuisance caused by neighbors' wood burning. We want to seek suitable solutions together with residents. A ban goes too far for us.

111724.explanation: The CDA wants to make waste collection simpler and fairer. The increasing number of illegal waste deposits costs us a lot of money. Therefore, we want to help residents clean up their living environment by introducing free collection services or neighborhood-focused 'clean-up actions'.

111729.explanation: The CDA currently sees no reason to allocate additional resources to local media.

111732.explanation: The CDA values associations. However, we cannot provide unlimited financial support to associations. Each situation must be assessed to determine if support is justified and, if so, under which conditions. Often it is not just about money, but mainly about support in navigating the maze of rules.

111735.explanation: The CDA believes everyone should be able to travel safely on the road to their home. Cameras are part of a 'total package'. We want to deploy cameras where they can make a targeted and appropriate contribution to increasing the safety of residents.

111737.explanation: The CDA wants to help starters find housing but believes that promoting loans ultimately pushes up prices. There are also sufficient solutions on the market that can help.

111740.explanation: The CDA believes that legalizing permanent residence in holiday parks poses a threat to tourism and recreational entrepreneurship. To solve the underlying shortage of permanent housing, simply new homes must be built.

111741.explanation: The CDA wants to allow small housing forms in rural areas if this strengthens the character of the green, open space, takes surrounding residents into account, and complies with applicable area conditions. We see no room for large-scale housing plans there.

111742.explanation: The CDA wants everyone who can, through work, to be valuable to their environment. Our main goal is not the delivery of the mentioned 'counter-performance.' It should primarily be aimed at increasing the chance of a real job.

111744.explanation: The CDA finds poverty reduction important but wants to address it broadly and not limit it to minimum groups with a focus on money. We also want more focus on properly implementing existing schemes rather than continually inventing new ones.

111747.explanation: The CDA finds it important to keep traffic pressure evenly distributed in our city. To stimulate flow, it is important to continue distinguishing between residential neighborhoods and through traffic on main roads.

111748.explanation: The CDA believes there are limits to what is still feasible and affordable in healthcare to continue providing for increasing care needs. We find the quality and/or availability of care more important than the person and/or free choice.

111750.explanation: The CDA considers reducing the property tax unrealistic. The tax constitutes an important source of income for the municipality. As a society, we expect a lot from the municipality, and an appropriate compensation should correspond. We ensure that residents' money is spent usefully.

111751.explanation: The CDA is neither for nor against abolishing the dog tax. If we abolish it, this simply results in a tax increase elsewhere. We need to be honest with residents about that.

111752.explanation: The CDA does not want to raise the tourist tax again. We have already implemented an increase with recent budget cuts. Besides, it concerns only a handful of entrepreneurs and tourists. As a city, we want to become and remain attractive.

111755.explanation: The CDA wants to adhere to national and regional agreements regarding the allocation of status holders to social rented housing. We do nothing more or less than necessary.

111757.explanation: For the CDA, completely banning solar fields is not an option. In our environmental vision and sustainability goals, we have agreed to allow solar fields. However, we want them to be integrated into the landscape and removed after their lifespan.

111758.explanation: The CDA wants to maintain the current swimming facility. Renewal is not currently an option.

111760.explanation: The CDA believes every child deserves a chance to learn to swim. However, offering swim lessons is not a municipal task. Most parents are perfectly capable of arranging this themselves and do so. Where parents truly cannot afford it, municipal schemes can be considered.

111761.explanation: The CDA finds there are sufficient coffeeshops in our municipality.

111763.explanation: The CDA can agree with high-rise buildings (> 5 residential floors) provided they fit well at the location. Space for building is limited and the demand for affordable housing is considerable. By building compactly, more space remains outside the city for nature and recreation.

111764.explanation: The CDA finds a community center per neighborhood too costly. We prefer to focus on better and multifunctional use of existing community centers in West and East. In the city center, cultural heritage can fulfill this function. We also think it is much smarter to look at school buildings.

111766.explanation: The CDA wants sufficient parking space for residents in every neighborhood. But we also see that space in our city is scarce. National developments should help us find a good balance.

111768.explanation: The CDA takes a positive approach to wind turbines and wants to consider future generations. Realization is not an end in itself and must meet clear conditions. We will not agree to new wind turbines if the nuisance to nearby residents has not been considered.

111769.explanation: The CDA has long advocated preserving the petting zoo at its current location. It has proposed multiple ideas to reduce the annual costs for the municipality. It is unfortunate that nothing has been done about this so far.

111770.explanation: The CDA supports the construction of a bicycle connection over the Lek. Despite the 'economic' benefits, we currently do not find it responsible to pay for it from our 'own' funds. The bill for realization and management should be fully borne by the national government and the involved provinces.

111712.explanation: We are strong supporters of this; a good example is that residents can express themselves for or against the arrival of new wind turbines.

111715.explanation: The municipality must accommodate farmers benevolently in this but judge each case individually.

111716.explanation: This way residents gain more say over the energy transition.

111719.explanation: This creates a vulnerable dependency; if a seasonal worker loses their job, they are more likely to become homeless.

111721.explanation: Residents who burn wood should be mindful of their neighbors, but not every stove or fireplace categorically causes nuisance or health damage.

111724.explanation: Not everyone has the possibility to transport bulky waste to the landfill themselves.

111729.explanation: A local broadcaster deserves support but must remain journalistically independent.

111732.explanation: Sport is healthy and fosters connection; the municipality must contribute to this.

111735.explanation: Camera surveillance offers no guarantees for safety and privacy must be handled carefully.

111737.explanation: This has the opposite effect. Without a larger supply, this only drives up prices further. Moreover, there is primarily a dire shortage of affordable rental housing.

111740.explanation: This can also help to counter the housing crisis.

111741.explanation: We must preserve the green environment as much as possible, while keeping an eye on the deep housing crisis and steady population growth.

111742.explanation: The benefit is a social right, which people have contributed to during their working lives. Beneficiaries can decide for themselves how to spend their time.

111744.explanation: Poverty in the Netherlands is significant and people urgently need this support.

111747.explanation: Not every road within built-up areas is unsuitable for 50 km/h.

111748.explanation: We should accommodate people as much as possible in this. However, due to shortages in healthcare and the complexity of tenders, sometimes people will have to comply.

111750.explanation: This currently stands in Culemborg at about the national average.

111751.explanation: This covers the costs of facilities and nuisance.

111755.explanation: If people have the right to stay here, they must also integrate and participate in our society as quickly as possible. The municipality must make progress in the availability of social rental housing for everyone to also facilitate the housing of status holders.

111757.explanation: The municipality must always critically assess whether plans do not unacceptably affect the landscape, but can also approve in specific cases.

111758.explanation: A public facility for swimming lessons, etc. is important. The swimming pool must remain usable and safe, but must also fulfill its purpose.

111760.explanation: Swimming is important for the self-reliance of children and is also great fun. Organising it through schools also takes a lot of worries off the parents' hands.

111761.explanation: The municipality must assess each case according to the applicable rules.

111763.explanation: Such apartment buildings must fit the city’s appearance, but can make an important contribution to solving the housing crisis.

111764.explanation: Community centres are good for social cohesion in the city and support the community.

111766.explanation: This should be assessed per neighbourhood based on need.

111768.explanation: Residents of the entire Culemborg must express their opinion on this in a referendum. With a favorable outcome, the wind turbines will only be installed with the consent of local residents, and on the condition that inhabitants of Culemborg can benefit from the revenues.

111769.explanation: It is important that there remains a public place where children can interact with animals through play.

111770.explanation: The cycling bridge will be an enrichment of the city, but the province and national government must contribute. The municipality should not want to realise the bridge at any cost.

statements.title: Wind turbines may be built in the meadows around Culemborg.

titleParts.text: In de weilanden rond Culemborg mogen

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The residents of Culemborg must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Culemborg moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain allowed in Culemborg.

titleParts.text: In Culemborg moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in Culemborg.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The bicycle bridge over the Lek must be built anyway, even if it becomes more expensive.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: More tall residential apartment buildings may be built in Culemborg.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
Culemborg must renovate the De Waterlinie swimming pool.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should block the opening of a new coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The De Heuvel children's farm must be preserved, even if it costs the municipality extra money.
